title: Containers archive endpoint does not include x-docker-container-path-stat header
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
pkg/api/handlers/compat/containers_archive.go should include the header x-docker-container-path-stat in the response.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. curl -v .../containers/{name}/archive?path=/etc/motd
2. Review output for x-docker-container-path-stat header

**Describe the results you received:**
Header not seen.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Header in output

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
as of 3.0.2
```

comments.body:
@jwhonce I wanted to look at this issue but output from newest podman indicates this header is present:

```
$ curl -v --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock http://d/v3.0.0/containers/f2a52dc2a82c/archive?path=/etc/motd -o motd
*   Trying /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock:0...
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
  0     0    0     0    0     0      0      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--     0* Connected to d (/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock) port 80 (#0)
> GET /v3.0.0/containers/f2a52dc2a82c/archive?path=/etc/motd HTTP/1.1
> Host: d
> User-Agent: curl/7.74.0
> Accept: */*
> 
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Api-Version: 1.40
< Content-Type: application/x-tar
< Libpod-Api-Version: 3.1.0-dev
< Server: Libpod/3.1.0-dev (linux)
< X-Docker-Container-Path-Stat: eyJuYW1lIjoibW90ZCIsInNpemUiOjI4MywibW9kZSI6NDIwLCJtdGltZSI6IjIwMTktMDYtMTdUMTE6MDA6MTYrMDI6MDAiLCJpc0RpciI6ZmFsc2UsImxpbmtUYXJnZXQiOiIvZXRjL21vdGQifQ==
< Date: Sat, 13 Mar 2021 00:02:55 GMT
< Content-Length: 2048
< 
{ [2048 bytes data]
100  2048  100  2048    0     0  15058      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:-- 15058
* Connection #0 to host d left intact
```

`X-Docker-Container-Path-Stat: eyJuYW1lIjoibW90ZCIsInNp...` is the thing I think.
I'd also be surprised if we regressed on that since we're testing that in the `apiv2` tests. @jwhonce, did you myabe use an old version?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
